<!-- Rust Code Studio template — a short pre-design concept note from /brainstorm. Keep it to one page; no code, no type signatures, no file trees. -->

# Concept: <name>

*One sentence: what this is.*

---

## Problem / opportunity

*What hurts today, or what becomes possible. Why now. Keep it concrete — a scenario, not an abstraction.*

## Who it's for

*The user/consumer of this crate or feature (a downstream crate, a CLI user, a service). What they're trying to do.*

## The idea (one paragraph)

*The proposed direction in prose. The shape of the solution, not its implementation. What changes for the user.*

## Scope

**In:**
- *...*

**Out (explicitly, for now):**
- *...*

## Constraints & unknowns

*MSRV, no_std, async runtime, performance budget, semver commitments, dependencies you must / must not pull in. List the open questions that need answering before design.*

## Options weighed

*The 2–4 directions considered and why this one. One line each.*

| Option | Trade-off | Verdict |
|--------|-----------|---------|
| *A* | *...* | *chosen / rejected because…* |

## Chosen direction & next step

*The agreed direction (after sign-off) and which skill takes it forward: `/design-api`, `/architecture`, or `/dev-task` (solo) for a spike.*
